superstarBaseballCodes.title: Working Superstar Baseball Codes
superstarBaseballCodes.subtitle: Redeem codes for free Spins, Lucky Spins, and Coins. This is the fastest way to improve your first loadout without grinding matches.
superstarBaseballCodes.intro: Superstar Baseball codes are the clearest repeat-search intent for the game because they directly feed the Spins system that unlocks offensive and defensive styles. Open the Settings menu, scroll to the code field, paste a code exactly as written, and redeem it before it expires.
redeemSteps: Tap the Settings gear icon in the lower-left menu
Scroll to the bottom of the settings panel
Paste the code exactly as shown — codes are case-sensitive
Press Redeem to claim your free reward

superstarBaseballBeginnerGuide.title: How to Start Strong in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the core loop, the three roles, and the first decisions that matter most. New players get more value from early codes and clean fundamentals than from random grinding.
superstarBaseballBeginnerGuide.intro: Superstar Baseball is a 7v7 Roblox arcade baseball game where you pitch, bat, and field in fast matches. The smartest early-game path is simple: redeem codes first, use your opening spins, learn the strike zone, and build around the best pull you get instead of spreading resources everywhere.

steps.title: Redeem codes before your first real session
steps.description: Codes give free Spins, Lucky Spins, and Coins, which immediately speed up your first build.

steps.title: Use your opening four free spins
steps.description: New accounts start with four free spins to roll one offensive style and one defensive style.

steps.title: Understand your three jobs on the field
steps.description: You can play as Batter, Pitcher, or Fielder, and every role matters in a 7v7 match.

steps.title: Learn the four pitch types early
steps.description: Pitchers use Curveball, Sinker, Slider, and Fastball, each with different speed and movement.

steps.title: Stop swinging at everything
steps.description: The strike zone is tighter than it looks. Waiting for a real hittable pitch is more effective than spam swinging.

steps.title: Time swings late rather than early
steps.description: Early swings produce more bad contact, while late timing is more forgiving as you learn the release.

steps.title: Save Lucky Spins for upgrades that matter
steps.description: Lucky Spins have better odds than regular Spins, so use them when you want a stronger roster jump.

steps.title: Place your best hitter and pitcher in high-impact spots
steps.description: Build around your best pull, bat your strongest hitter in the 3rd or 4th slot, and start your best pitcher in important games.

quickTips: You earn one free spin every 30 minutes of play — stay consistent.
Lucky Spins have published odds of 0.5% Legendary, 2% Epic, 35% Rare, and 62.5% Uncommon.
A walk puts you on base just like a hit — do not chase every pitch.
Coordinated 7v7 teams win more consistently than solo-queue groups that don't communicate.

superstarBaseballBestStyles.title: What to Prioritize When Chasing the Best Styles
superstarBaseballBestStyles.subtitle: The strongest practical meta page is a priority grid showing what players should keep, chase, and spend on first.
superstarBaseballBestStyles.intro: For Superstar Baseball, the most reliable public data around styles is how you obtain them and how to evaluate the value of a pull. Lucky Spins improve your odds of rarer pulls, Legendary and Epic styles are the premium chase, and the best real-world decision is usually to invest around the strongest hitter or pitcher you already rolled instead of rerolling endlessly.

tiers.label: Lucky Spin upgrades
details: Lucky Spins have better odds than regular Spins.
Best target for players actively hunting stronger offensive or defensive upgrades.
Most efficient when used after redeeming codes and understanding what your lineup still lacks.

tiers.label: Legendary and Epic results
details: Published rarity odds list Legendary at 0.5% and Epic at 2%.
These are the styles worth building around instead of instantly rerolling.
Put your best hitter in the 3rd or 4th slot and your best pitcher into important matches.

tiers.label: Rare styles from regular grinding
details: Rare styles sit at 35% in the published rarity breakdown.
Good enough to anchor early and mid-game play while you stockpile better spins.
Best for players who want stable value without burning every Lucky Spin immediately.

tiers.label: Uncommon stopgaps
details: Uncommon styles are the most common published result at 62.5%.
Fine for learning batting, pitching, and fielding fundamentals.
Replace these first once you land a stronger offensive or defensive upgrade.

superstarBaseballOffensiveStyles.title: How to Evaluate Offensive Styles in Real Matches
superstarBaseballOffensiveStyles.subtitle: Players want to know what actually translates into runs. This module ties offensive decision-making to proven batting rules, timing windows, and scoring patterns.
superstarBaseballOffensiveStyles.intro: The most useful offensive page for Superstar Baseball is a practical reference built around how runs are actually created. Timing is the core skill, home runs are the fastest way to score, pitch recognition matters immediately, and your strongest offensive pull should shape lineup order.

cards.title: Power-first offense
cards.description: Prioritize offensive pulls that help you convert center-high pitches into extra-base damage, because home runs are the fastest way to score.
highlights: Best when you can already time fastballs cleanly
Works especially well in the 3rd or 4th batting slot
Punishes mistakes in the middle of the zone

cards.title: Timing and contact offense
cards.description: If your swings are still inconsistent, value offensive setups that reward cleaner timing over all-out aggression.
highlights: Late contact is safer than early flailing
Useful for new players learning the release window
Turns more at-bats into playable contact

cards.title: Pitch-reading offense
cards.description: The best offensive decisions start before the swing. Fastballs arrive straight and fast, while curves need a slight delay.
highlights: Reading the pitch in the first half-second creates better contact

cards.title: Patient plate approach
cards.description: Not every offensive situation should end with a swing. Walks still create base runners and force weak pitchers to challenge the zone.
highlights: Useful against pitchers who miss the zone
Pairs well with lineup spots that need steady on-base pressure

cards.title: Lineup-fit offense
cards.description: A strong offensive pull becomes more valuable when it is used in the right lineup slot instead of being buried randomly.
highlights: Let weaker bats support rather than carry run production
Build the order around your strongest pull

superstarBaseballDefensiveStyles.title: Superstar Baseball Defensive Styles
superstarBaseballDefensiveStyles.subtitle: Defense is easiest to undervalue when most players focus on batting first. Good defensive style choices help you secure cleaner catches, faster outs, and fewer extra bases allowed.
superstarBaseballDefensiveStyles.intro: Superstar Baseball starts players with one offensive style and one defensive style from spins, and defensive impact shows up most clearly in catching reliability, throw quality, positioning, and run prevention.

tiers.label: Reliable Catch + Fast Throw
details: The strongest defensive outcomes come from securing playable balls and turning them into quick outs.
Best for outfield recovery and stopping doubles from becoming triples.
Most valuable in pressure plays with runners on base.

tiers.label: Range + Positioning
details: Getting to the ball earlier reduces panic throws and gives the defense more time to make the correct play.
Useful for covering gaps and cutting off line drives.
Helps back up teammates on shallow flies and awkward bounces.

tiers.label: Pure Arm Strength
details: A strong arm helps after the catch, but does less on its own if your team is late to the ball.
Best for long throws from the outfield and force plays at home.
More impactful when paired with reliable catching fundamentals.

tiers.label: Low-Impact Utility Defense
tiers.summary: Playable but not a priority target
details: Defensive value drops when a setup does not clearly improve catch consistency or throw speed.
Fine for casual play or early roster depth.
Replace these first once you land a better defensive upgrade.

superstarBaseballControls.title: Superstar Baseball Controls
superstarBaseballControls.subtitle: Most new players lose early games because they do not know the key inputs fast enough. This page gives a quick-reference control list for batting, pitching, and fielding on PC.
superstarBaseballControls.intro: Superstar Baseball splits gameplay into batter, pitcher, and fielder roles. The most important confirmed PC inputs are swing, pitch selection, dive, and throw, with pitch selection mapped to number keys and defensive throws requiring aim toward a teammate.

rows.notes: Use to make contact at the plate.
Use for catch attempts and emergency reach plays.
After catching the ball, aim the green arrow toward the target teammate.
Open Settings, scroll down, and enter a reward code.

superstarBaseballPitchingGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Pitching Guide
superstarBaseballPitchingGuide.subtitle: Pitching becomes the first real skill check once players understand the controls. Winning more often comes from mixing speed, movement, and location instead of repeating the same throw.
superstarBaseballPitchingGuide.intro: The game currently has four confirmed pitch types: Curveball at 65-85 mph, Sinker at 70-90 mph, Slider at 75-90 mph, and Fastball at 80-100 mph. The best practical approach is to use each pitch for a different purpose and sequence them so the batter cannot lock into one timing window.

steps.title: Learn what each pitch is trying to do
steps.description: Fastball wins with speed, Curveball changes eye level and movement, Slider adds sharper late break, and Sinker is useful for weaker contact and ground-ball pressure.

steps.title: Start with speed, then break timing
steps.description: A common sequence is hard pitch first, then a slower or moving pitch. Fastball inside followed by Curveball outside makes batters commit early.

steps.title: Use Sinker when you want softer contact
steps.description: Sinkers drop and are especially useful when you want hitters to roll over the ball instead of driving it cleanly.

steps.title: Avoid repeating the same pattern
steps.description: Throwing the same pitch in the same situation makes the at-bat easier to read. Rotate locations and avoid becoming predictable in full counts.

steps.title: Build a simple three-pitch plan
steps.description: A strong basic pattern is Fastball inside, Curveball outside, then a lower off-speed or breaking look. The goal is three uncomfortable decisions in a row.

pitchReference.name: Curveball
pitchReference.use: Eye level change and late movement

superstarBaseballBattingGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Batting Guide
superstarBaseballBattingGuide.subtitle: Batting looks simple at first, but timing and swing discipline separate weak contact from real scoring pressure.
superstarBaseballBattingGuide.intro: The batting window in Superstar Baseball is tight, and timing is the core mechanic to master. A strong baseline is to watch the ball out of the pitcher's hand, avoid panic swings, and look for center to upper-zone contact when trying to drive the ball for extra bases.

steps.title: Learn the strike zone before chasing power
steps.description: The strike zone plays smaller than many new players expect, so patient at-bats create better swing opportunities than constant early swings.

steps.title: Start your swing around the halfway point
steps.description: A practical timing cue is to begin the swing when the ball is about halfway to the plate. Too early often leads to foul contact, and too late leads to whiffs or weak grounders.

steps.title: Stop spam swinging
steps.description: Repeated panic swings make it easier for pitchers to beat you with movement or speed changes. Let borderline pitches go and force a better ball into your zone.

steps.title: Hunt center-high contact for power
steps.description: When players want more home-run or extra-base threat, center-high zone contact is the most useful target area to practice.

steps.title: Read the pitcher, not just the ball
steps.description: Pitchers fall into habits. If you notice repeated fastballs in pressure spots or the same breaking pitch after a strike, sit on that pattern and punish it.

superstarBaseballHowToHitHomeRuns.title: How to Hit More Home Runs in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballHowToHitHomeRuns.subtitle: The cleanest power comes from pitch selection, contact height, and timing at the plate. This guide turns the game's batting tips into a repeatable home run routine.
superstarBaseballHowToHitHomeRuns.intro: The most reliable power advice across current guides is consistent: wait for a hittable pitch, attack the center-high part of the zone, and connect as the ball reaches the plate. If you keep producing weak grounders, your contact point is too low or too late.

steps.title: Wait for a hittable pitch
steps.description: Do not swing at everything. Current guides stress that patience wins at-bats, and a walk still gets you on base.

steps.title: Hunt the center-high zone
steps.description: The best current home run tip is to attack pitches in the center-high part of the strike zone because that gives you the best launch angle for deep contact.

steps.title: Swing as the ball reaches the plate
steps.description: Timing matters more than swing spam. The recommended contact window is right as the ball arrives at the plate, not early.

steps.title: Use bad contact as feedback
steps.description: If you keep hitting grounders, adjust upward or later. Weak ground balls are tied to low contact or late timing.

steps.title: Build around your best hitter
steps.description: When you roll a strong offensive character or style, make that bat the center of your lineup. Put your best hitter in the 3rd or 4th slot for maximum RBI chances.

superstarBaseballFieldingGuide.title: Fielding Fundamentals That Save Runs
superstarBaseballFieldingGuide.subtitle: Clean positioning, accurate throws, and disciplined use of the dive input separate solid teams from chaotic ones.
superstarBaseballFieldingGuide.intro: The core defensive role in Superstar Baseball is straightforward: fielders catch the ball and make accurate throws to get runners out. Start with reliable outs first, then layer in faster reactions and better team coordination.

items.title: Know the fielder's job
items.description: Your defensive role is to catch the ball and make accurate throws to retire runners.

items.title: Use dive only when the play demands it
items.description: The Dive key is on E. Treat it as a recovery tool for hard-hit balls and low catches, not your default fielding input.

items.title: Aim every throw
items.description: After a catch, throw with left click and line up the green arrow with your target teammate for a cleaner relay.

items.title: Protect the easy out first
items.description: A simple, on-target out is worth more than a rushed highlight attempt. Reliable throws keep innings under control.

items.title: Communicate on team defense
items.description: Current strategy coverage says coordinated teams win more often, especially on defense.

items.title: Invest in fielding-impact upgrades before cosmetics
items.description: Spend coins on upgrades that improve win rate first, including stronger fielding support, before cosmetic purchases.

superstarBaseballBaseRunningGuide.title: Base Running Basics for New Superstar Baseball Players
superstarBaseballBaseRunningGuide.subtitle: Good base running starts before you leave the bag. Patience, clean reads, and smart aggression create more runs than constant forcing.
superstarBaseballBaseRunningGuide.intro: The available game coverage points to a clear practical approach: get on base through patient at-bats, respect how quickly defenders can convert outs, and push extra bases when the play is clearly in your favor.

steps.title: Create runners with patience first
steps.description: You do not have to swing at every pitch. Current batting guidance specifically calls out that a walk puts you on base just like a hit.

steps.title: Read the ball before forcing extra bases
steps.description: The safest early habit is to confirm clean contact and defensive positioning before committing to extra advancement.

steps.title: Let speed-oriented playstyles matter
steps.description: Superstar Baseball playstyles improve batting, pitching, and base running, so runner value is part of progression.

steps.title: Push harder when your team is coordinated
steps.description: Coordinated groups control the field better than solo play. Aggressive advancement is safer when teammates are reading the play together.

steps.title: Protect close-game outs
steps.description: New players lose tight games by giving away easy outs on the bases. Favor clean advancement over low-percentage gambles until you know the pace of the match.

superstarBaseballSpinsGuide.title: How Spins Work in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballSpinsGuide.subtitle: Spins are the core progression loop for styles, characters, gear, and cosmetics. Knowing which spin to use now and which one to save gives you better value immediately.
superstarBaseballSpinsGuide.intro: Superstar Baseball starts new players with free spins and ties long-term progression to the Spins system. Lucky Spins are the better resource for stronger pulls, and Style Spins are for cosmetics rather than gameplay power.

faqs.question: What spin types are confirmed right now?
faqs.answer: Current guide coverage confirms three main categories: Spins (basic roll currency), Lucky Spins (better odds for rarer rewards), and Style Spins (cosmetic unlocks only).

faqs.question: What should I spend first?
faqs.answer: Use regular Spins freely because they are the easiest to replace through play and codes. Save Lucky Spins for moments when you want a better shot at stronger characters or gear.

faqs.question: What do new players get at the start?
faqs.answer: You begin with four free spins to roll one offensive style and one defensive style. You also receive one free spin every 30 minutes of play.

faqs.question: What are the currently published style odds?
faqs.answer: The current beginner guide lists Lucky Odds as: Legendary 0.5%, Epic 2%, Rare 35%, and Uncommon 62.5%.

faqs.question: What is the fastest way to get more Spins?
faqs.answer: Codes are the fastest source right now. Active codes include DEADEYE, 90KLIKES, SOFTBALL, SKYBOUND, BLIGHT, TUTORIAL, and SUPERSTAR, with rewards that include free Spins, Lucky Spins, and Coins.

faqs.question: Where do I redeem codes?
faqs.answer: Open Superstar Baseball, tap the Settings gear, scroll to the bottom of the settings menu, enter the reward code, and press Redeem. Codes are case-sensitive.

superstarBaseballHowToGetMoreSpins.title: How to Get More Spins in Superstar Baseball
superstarBaseballHowToGetMoreSpins.subtitle: Spins are the fastest way to improve your loadout because they unlock new Offensive and Defensive Styles. The quickest gains come from stacking codes first, then adding match play and milestone progress.
superstarBaseballHowToGetMoreSpins.intro: If your goal is to reroll into better styles faster, start with the guaranteed sources before relying on grind. Codes are the best immediate source, while match play and milestone progress are the repeatable in-game path after that.

items.label: Redeem the latest active reward codes first
details: DEADEYE — 5 Spins
90KLIKES — 2 Spins
SOFTBALL — 5 Spins
SKYBOUND — 5 Spins
BLIGHT — 5 Lucky Spins
TUTORIAL — 3 Lucky Spins
SUPERSTAR — 100 Coins

items.label: Prioritize Lucky Spins when available
details: Lucky Spins have better odds for rarer styles.
BLIGHT and TUTORIAL are current public code sources for Lucky Spins.

items.label: Play matches to keep spin income moving after codes
details: Community guide coverage states you earn Spins through playing matches.
Solo queue is available in 7v7, so you do not need a premade team to keep farming.

items.label: Push milestones for extra spin progress
details: Milestones are an ongoing source of Spins after code redemption.
This is the main fallback once the current code batch is exhausted.

items.label: Check update windows for fresh code drops
details: Code trackers note that new codes often appear around updates.
That makes update days the best time to sweep for new free Spins.

items.label: Use the in-game redeem path correctly
details: Open Settings from the lower-left area of the screen.
Scroll to the reward code field, enter the code exactly, and press Redeem.
Codes are case-sensitive in public redemption instructions.

superstarBaseballCoinsGuide.title: Superstar Baseball Coins Guide
superstarBaseballCoinsGuide.subtitle: Coins are the main currency tied to store spending and early account value. Code rewards are the easiest no-cost way to start building a coin balance.
superstarBaseballCoinsGuide.intro: For practical progression, think of coins as your store budget: small code injections and bonuses help early, while premium perks add larger one-time value.

rows.notes: The game page advertises a 500 COIN BONUS for joining Metavision.
One active public code currently gives direct coin value instead of Spins.
Included alongside exclusive skins and 2X XP.
Coins can be spent on purchases from the Store.
Coins are the main currency for upgrades and cosmetics.

superstarBaseballGamepasses.title: Superstar Baseball Gamepasses
superstarBaseballGamepasses.subtitle: The two tracked gamepasses serve different goals: faster account value through VIP perks, or team-control priority through Team Captain.
superstarBaseballGamepasses.intro: If you only plan to buy one pass, decide whether you want direct value or team-control priority. Superstars VIP is the stronger all-around package for players who want bonus XP, cosmetics, and a coin injection.

passes.name: Superstars VIP
passes.bestFor: Players who want the most overall account value
perks: Exclusive legendary Superstar Slugger bat skin

passes.name: Team Captain
passes.bestFor: Players who want captain priority in team selection
perks: Automatically picked over non-pass holders to become a Team Captain

superstarBaseballUpdates.title: Superstar Baseball Updates and Current Status
superstarBaseballUpdates.subtitle: This page tracks the latest visible state of the game, from official update dates to current code waves and live community growth.
superstarBaseballUpdates.intro: Superstar Baseball is still actively maintained and has a large live audience footprint across Roblox and community tracking pages. Combine official update markers with current code batches and live ecosystem stats.

entries.title: Official Roblox page shows a fresh update
entries.description: The Roblox experience listing currently shows the game as updated on March 31, 2026, which makes it the main official marker for the newest visible version state.

entries.title: Current active code batch remains live
entries.description: Public code trackers confirmed the active set on April 1, 2026, including DEADEYE, 90KLIKES, SOFTBALL, SKYBOUND, BLIGHT, TUTORIAL, and SUPERSTAR.

entries.title: Recent code era reflects ongoing milestone and themed drops
entries.description: The current and recently expired code pool includes milestone tags like 90KLIKES, 80KLIKES, 70KLIKES, 60KLIKES, and 50KLIKES, plus themed drops such as BLIGHT, SKYBOUND, and SOFTBALL.

entries.title: Tracked ecosystem shows strong community scale
entries.description: Metavision's Roblox group is listed at 656,244 members with strong previous-day growth on Rolimon's, showing strong current community momentum around the developer.

entries.title: Live game footprint remains substantial
entries.description: Public tracking pages list release information from January 2025 and show a large ongoing player base with over 42 million total visits and tens of thousands of concurrent players.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Superstar Baseball

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Open Superstar Baseball, tap the Settings gear icon in the lower-left menu, scroll to the bottom to find the code field, paste the code exactly as shown, and press Redeem. Codes are case-sensitive.

questions.question: What are the active Superstar Baseball codes right now?
questions.answer: The currently active codes are DEADEYE (5 spins), 90KLIKES (2 spins), SOFTBALL (5 lucky spins), SKYBOUND (5 spins), BLIGHT (5 lucky spins), TUTORIAL (3 lucky spins), and SUPERSTAR (100 coins).

questions.question: What is the difference between Spins and Lucky Spins in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Regular Spins are the basic roll currency. Lucky Spins have better odds for rarer styles, with published odds of 0.5% Legendary, 2% Epic, 35% Rare, and 62.5% Uncommon.

questions.question: How do I get more Spins in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Redeem active codes first for the fastest gains. After that, earn Spins by playing matches and completing milestones. You also receive one free spin every 30 minutes of play.

questions.question: What are the pitch types in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: There are four confirmed pitch types: Curveball (65-85 mph, number key 1), Sinker (70-90 mph, key 2), Slider (75-90 mph, key 3), and Fastball (80-100 mph, key 4).

questions.question: How do I hit home runs in Superstar Baseball?
questions.answer: Wait for a hittable pitch, aim for the center-high zone, and swing as the ball reaches the plate. Weak grounders usually mean contact is too low or timing is too late. Place your best hitter in the 3rd or 4th batting slot.
